title: Atomic Fact-Checking Increases Clinician Trust in Large Language Model Recommendations for Oncology Decision Support: A Randomized Controlled Trial
summary:
Question: Does atomic fact-checking, which decomposes AI treatment recommendations into individually verifiable claims linked to source guideline documents, increase clinician trust compared to traditional explainability approaches?
  Findings: In this randomized trial of 356 clinicians generating 7,476 trust ratings, atomic fact-checking produced a large effect on trust (Cohen's d = 0.94), increasing the proportion of clinicians expressing trust from 26.9% to 66.5%. Traditional transparency mechanisms showed a dose-response gradient of improvement over baseline (d = 0.25 to 0.50).
  Meaning: Decomposing AI recommendations into individually verifiable claims linked to source guidelines produces substantially higher clinician trust than traditional explainability approaches in high-stakes clinical decisions.
comment: 28 pages, 5 figures, 2 tables, supplement will be made available upon original publication

AI.tldr: 本文通过随机试验对比了原子事实核查（将AI治疗建议分解为可单独验证并链接来源指南的主张）与传统可解释性方法对临床医生信任AI诊疗建议的影响，发现原子事实核查能大幅提升医生信任度。
AI.motivation: AI在临床诊疗中应用越来越广泛，而传统AI可解释性方法提升临床医生信任的效果有限，需要验证更有效的可验证解释方式对医生信任的提升作用。
AI.method: 开展随机对照试验，招募356名临床医生生成7476份信任评级，对比原子事实核查与传统透明度机制对医生信任AI治疗建议的影响，用Cohen's d衡量效应量。
AI.result: 原子事实核查对信任的提升效应量大（Cohen's d = 0.94），将信任AI的医生比例从26.9%提升至66.5%；传统透明度机制相较于基线仅呈现小到中等的梯度改善（d = 0.25至0.50）。
AI.conclusion: 在高风险临床决策场景中，将AI建议分解为链接源指南的可单独验证主张的原子事实核查方法，相较于传统可解释性方法能显著提高临床医生的信任。

title: Beyond Similarity Search: A Unified Data Layer for Production RAG Systems
summary: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems have become the standard architecture for grounding large language models in organizational knowledge. Yet production deployments consistently expose a gap between clean prototype performance and real-world reliability. This paper identifies three root causes of that gap: data staleness, tenant data leakage, and query composition explosion. All three trace back to the conventional split-system data layer. We propose and evaluate a unified data layer built on PostgreSQL with native vector search (pgvector) and HNSW indexing. Controlled benchmarks on 50,000 documents show 92% latency reduction for date-filtered queries, 74% for tenant-scoped queries, zero synchronization inconsistency, and complete elimination of cross-tenant data leakage with 93% less synchronization code. We additionally discuss a recommended hybrid tier architecture

AI.tldr: 本文指出现有检索增强生成（RAG）系统在原型性能和实际可靠性之间存在差距，提出基于带原生向量搜索的PostgreSQL（pgvector）和HNSW索引构建统一数据层解决该问题，并通过基准测试验证了其优势，还讨论了推荐的混合分层架构。
AI.motivation: RAG已经成为将大语言模型对接组织知识的标准架构，但生产部署中一直存在干净原型性能和实际可靠性之间的差距，需要找到该差距的根本原因并解决问题。
AI.method: 首先定位出传统分裂系统数据层导致该差距的三个根本原因：数据陈旧、租户数据泄露和查询组合爆炸；然后提出并评估基于带原生向量搜索pgvector和HNSW索引的PostgreSQL构建的统一数据层，在50000个文档上进行受控基准测试验证其性能。
AI.result: 基准测试结果显示，该统一数据层将日期过滤查询的延迟降低了92%，租户范围查询延迟降低了74%，实现了零同步不一致，彻底消除了跨租户数据泄露，同时同步代码减少了93%。
AI.conclusion: 传统分裂系统数据层是导致RAG系统实际部署性能缺口的根源，基于PostgreSQL原生向量搜索构建的统一数据层可以有效解决这三个根本问题，大幅提升RAG系统的实际可靠性并降低 overhead，文中还进一步推荐了混合分层架构。

title: APEX: Large-scale Multi-task Aesthetic-Informed Popularity Prediction for AI-Generated Music
summary: Music popularity prediction has attracted growing research interest, with relevance to artists, platforms, and recommendation systems. However, the explosive rise of AI-generated music platforms has created an entirely new and largely unexplored landscape, where a surge of songs is produced and consumed daily without the traditional markers of artist reputation or label backing. Key, yet unexplored in this pursuit is aesthetic quality. We propose APEX, the first large-scale multi-task learning framework for AI-generated music, trained on over 211k songs (10k hours of audio) from Suno and Udio, that jointly predicts engagement-based popularity signals - streams and likes scores - alongside five perceptual aesthetic quality dimensions from frozen audio embeddings extracted from MERT, a self-supervised music understanding model. Aesthetic quality and popularity capture complementary aspects of music that together prove valuable: in an out-of-distribution evaluation on the Music Arena dataset, comprising pairwise human preference battles across eleven generative music systems unseen during training, including aesthetic features consistently improves preference prediction, demonstrating strong generalisation of the learned representations across generative architectures.

AI.tldr: 本文针对AI生成音乐场景下缺少传统 popularity 标记且未探索美学质量作用的音乐 popularity 预测问题，提出了首个大规模多任务学习框架APEX，验证了结合美学质量能有效提升预测性能和跨模型泛化能力。
AI.motivation: 音乐流行度预测已有较多研究，但AI生成音乐平台爆发式增长带来了新场景：大量音乐每日产出消费，缺乏传统的艺术家声誉、厂牌支持等标记，且美学质量在该场景的音乐流行度预测中还未被探索，因此需要解决这个新问题。
AI.method: 提出APEX，首个面向AI生成音乐的大规模多任务学习框架，基于自监督音乐理解模型MERT提取的冻结音频嵌入，联合预测基于互动的流行度信号（播放量、点赞量）以及五个感知美学质量维度，训练数据包含来自Suno和Udio的超过21.1万首歌曲（1万小时音频），并在包含未见过的11种生成音乐系统的 pairwise 人类偏好数据的Music Arena数据集上做分布外评估。
AI.result: 实验结果显示，美学质量和流行度捕捉了音乐的互补信息，加入美学特征 consistently 提升了偏好预测性能，所学表示在不同生成架构上展现出很强的泛化能力。
AI.conclusion: 结合美学质量能有效提升AI生成音乐的流行度/偏好预测，APEX框架在跨生成音乐架构的泛化任务上表现优异，为AI生成音乐的 popularity 预测提供了有效的新方案。

title: TeamUp: Semantic Project Matching and Team Formation for Learning at Scale
summary: Project-based learning improves student engagement and learning outcomes, yet allocating students to appropriately challenging projects while forming cognitively diverse teams remains difficult at scale. Traditional allocation methods (manual spreadsheets, preference surveys) can't construct the cognitively diverse teams that that collaborate cognitively. This mismatch perpetuates equity issues: high-performing students self-select visible projects while under-represented students face reduced access to opportunity. We propose TeamUp, a lightweight, embedding-based team-forming system designed to improve learning outcomes and equity in large-scale project-based courses. TeamUp uses semantic embeddings from pretrained language models to match students to projects aligned with their skill level. The system employs a hybrid ranking algorithm combining cosine similarity with pedagogical constraints (difficulty alignment, domain preferences, and demand balancing) to generate personalised and transparent recommendations. Beyond individual matching, TeamUp constructs cognitively diverse teams by modelling skill complementarity through embedding variance, ensuring teams possess well-distributed capabilities rather than homogeneous strengths. We evaluated TeamUp through a virtual experiment using 250 student profiles and 60 project descriptions. Results show: (1) substantially higher match quality (mean cosine similarity of 0.74 vs. 0.43); (2) better difficulty alignment (83% placed within one level vs. 34%); (3) more diverse teams (82% covering three or more technical areas vs. 41%); and (4) sub-second recommendation latency at operational costs under $0.10 per student.

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一个用于大规模项目式学习的团队组建系统TeamUp，解决学生分配到合适挑战性项目和构建认知多样性团队的难题，经实验验证该系统在匹配质量、难度对齐、团队多样性和运行效率上都有显著提升。
AI.motivation: 项目式学习能提升学生参与度和学习成果，但大规模场景下难以将学生分配到适配难度的项目并组建认知多样化团队；传统分配方法无法构建能有效协作的认知多样化团队，还会加剧公平问题，优等生自选热门项目，少数群体学生机会减少，因此需要新的解决方法。
AI.method: TeamUp是基于嵌入的轻量系统：利用预训练语言模型得到的语义嵌入将学生匹配到符合其技能水平的项目；采用混合排序算法，结合余弦相似度与教学约束（难度对齐、领域偏好、需求平衡）生成个性化透明推荐；通过嵌入方差建模技能互补性来构建认知多样化团队，保证团队能力分布均衡而非同质化。通过250个学生画像和60个项目描述的虚拟实验评估系统。
AI.result: 实验结果显示，对比传统方法，TeamUp匹配质量更高（平均余弦相似度0.74 vs 0.43），难度对齐更好（83%学生分配在目标难度一级范围内 vs 34%），团队多样性更高（82%团队覆盖三个及以上技术领域 vs 41%），且推荐延迟低于一秒，每个学生运营成本低于0.1美元。
AI.conclusion: TeamUp能有效改善大规模项目式课程中的学习成果和公平性，在匹配质量、团队多样性等方面表现优异，同时运行成本低、效率高，适合大规模应用。
